OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT PETER SCHURMAN_S MOTION FOR STAY (TRANSACTION ID # 65962944) FILED BY PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO A MUNICIPAL CORPORATION THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA BY AND THROUGH DENNIS J. HERRERA, CITY ATTORNEY FOR THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANICSCO

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PETER SCHURMAN DEFENDANT PETER SCHURMAN'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR STAY IS DENIED IN PART AND GRANTED IN PART. DEFENDANT MUST FILE AN ANSWER TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, WHICH NEED NOT BE VERIFIED. (CODE CIV. PROC. SECTION 446(A) [WHEN A PUBLIC ENTITY IS PLAINTIFF, "THE ANSWER SHALL BE VERIFIED, UNLESS AN ADMISSION OF THE TRUTH OF THE COMPLAINT MIGHT SUBJECT THE PARTY TO A CRIMINAL PROSECUTION"].) DECAMP V. FIRST KENSINGTON CORPORATION (1978) 83 CAL.APP.3D 268 IS NOT TO THE CONTRARY. (SEE ID. AT 276 [NOTING THAT "A 1933 AMENDMENT LIMITED THE SELF-INCRIMINATION EXCEPTION SO AS TO APPLY ONLY TO CASES IN WHICH THE STATE OR AN OFFICER THEREOF WAS PLAINTIFF," AND HOLDING ONLY THAT "IN PRIVATE LITIGATION THE DEFENDANT IS NOT ENTITLED TO DEFEND A VERIFIED COMPLAINT UNLESS HE CAN DENY ITS ESSENTIAL ALLEGATIONS UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY" (EMPHASIS ADDED)].) THE COURT GRANTS AS UNOPPOSED A LIMITED STAY OF PROCEEDINGS AS TO DEFENDANT UNTIL FEBRUARY 15, 2021. IF DEFENDANT IS STILL SUBJECT TO CRIMINAL PROSECUTION AT THAT TIME, HE MAY RENEW HIS MOTION. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

MOTION TO STRIKE
Off Calendar Jan-28-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Renee Rosenblit, Esq. appearing for Plaintiff City and County of the San Francisco.
Hunter Sims, Esq. appearing for Plaintiff City and County of the San Francisco.
Wade Chow, Esq. observing for Plaintiff City and County of the San Francisco.
Matt Wertheim, Esq. representing Defendant Kevin O'Connor in limited scope.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Proceedings reported by Peggy Tsujimoto, CSR#5229; peggytsujimoto@gmail.com; (415)828-1919. (D501)

DEFENDANT KEVIN O'CONNOR MOTION TO STRIKE Plaintiff's Untimely Reply re: Motion for Attorney's Fees is not contested.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT KEVIN O'CONNOR MOTION TO STRIKE Plaintiff's Untimely Reply re: Motion for Attorney's Fees

is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient notice per CCP 1005. To the extent Defendant objects to the untimely filing of the reply in support of the motion for attorney's fees, the objection is overruled. Reply was timely filed and served on January 21, 2026. While that filing was rejected, the City immediately re-filed on January 22, 2025 correcting the hearing department number, Defendant points to no substantive difference between the two filings.

MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES
Off Calendar Jan-28-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Renee Rosenblit, Esq. appearing for Plaintiff City and County of the San Francisco.
Hunter Sims, Esq. appearing for Plaintiff City and County of the San Francisco.
Wade Chow, Esq. observing for Plaintiff City and County of the San Francisco.
Matt Wertheim, Esq. representing Defendant Kevin O'Connor in limited scope.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Proceedings reported by Peggy Tsujimoto, CSR#5229; peggytsujimoto@gmail.com; (415)828-1919. (D501)

Defendant's counsel contests the tentative ruling for the motion for attorney's fees, presents argument and proof at length.
The court inquires from plaintiff if the hours requested are pertaining to the this particular defendant.
Plaintiff confirms and provides proof.
Matter is argued.

Defendant O'Connor presents argument as to the tentative ruling on the motion to strike and/or tax costs by reading a statement in open court.
Plaintiff responds.
The matter is argued.

After hearing the proofs and arguments, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES

is GRANTED as requested for reasonable time at reasonable hourly rate incurred in litigating this action against defendants O'Connor.

Order is signed and filed this day.

Off Calendar Apr-18-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
RENEE E. ROSENBLIT, Esq., Deputy City Attorney (415) 554-3853 renee.rosenblit@sfcityatty.org Attorney for Plaintiffs CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO and PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA .
David A. Ericksen , Esq., Ericksen Legal, PC 415-652-4031 dae@elpc.law Attorney for Defendants Santos & Urrutia Associates, Inc et al

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling with modifications as follows:

PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO A MUNICIPAL CORPORATION, THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion By Plaintiffs To Have Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted Against Defendants Rodrigo Santos, Albert Urrutia, And Santos & Urrutia Associates, Inc.

Plaintiff the City and County of San Francisco's motion for deemed admissions and sanctions against defendants Rodrigo Santos, Albert Urrutia, and Santos & Urrutia Associates, Inc. The Court grants the motion in its entirety, except as to Santos & Urrutia Associates Inc., which has filed for bankruptcy and the automatic stay is in effect. The motion is denied without prejudice as to Santos & Urrutia Associates, Inc. (see word minutes for complete details)

Order is signed.

Off Calendar Mar-26-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances
RENEE E. ROSENBLIT, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, (415) 554-3853 renee.rosenblit@sfcityatty.org Attorney for Plaintiffs CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO and PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA
David A. Ericksen, Esq., Severson, 415-398-3344 amp@sevrson.com Attorney for Defendants Santos & Urrutia Associates, Inc., (via Zoom Conference)

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument modifies and adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO A MUNICIPAL CORPORATION, THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion To Compel Deposition Of Defendant Rodrigo Santos And Impose Sanctions

The City and County of San Francisco's unopposed request to compel the deposition of defendant Rodrigo Santos is granted. Some scheduling issues can be expected in light of the fact that Santos is in custody but the City has shown good cause for the deposition and it ultimately should occur. The City shall meet and confer with Santos's counsel within five court days after entry of this order to find a mutually agreeable date. The deposition shall occur by April 17, 2025, unless the parties otherwise agree. If meet and confer efforts do not result in a mutually agreeable date, or if Santos's counsel does not participate in meet and confer efforts, the City may, after five court days have passed, unilaterally set the deposition on any day convenient to the City.

The City seeks sanctions. CCP sec. 2025.450(g)(1) requires the Court to impose sanctions unless it finds the deponent acted with substantial justification or there are circumstances that render imposition of sanctions unjust. The court does not find that Santos acted with substantial justification but reduces the requested sanctions amount to reflect the difficulties of setting a deposition date under these circumstances. Mr. Santos to bear the costs of recording his deposition in its entirety and must make a timely payment to the court reporter and videographer.

Plaintiff City and County of San Franciso to prepare and submit a proposed order on compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Deposition For Alex Santos
Off Calendar Mar-12-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO A MUNICIPAL CORPORATION, THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA Notice Of Motion To Compel Deposition For Alex Santos

Plaintiff City & County of San Francisco's unopposed motion to compel third-party witness Alex Santos to comply with its subpoena for deposition testimony and documents is granted. The Rosenblit declaration demonstrates that the City served the subpoena (Ex. G) and the witness did not appear (Ex. H). The City's counsel attempted to contact Santos's attorney when Santos did not appear. (Rosenblit Dec. para. 13.) The prerequisites for relief under CCP 2025.450 are satisfied.

Accordingly, the court orders Santos to appear for deposition and produce the documents specified in the subpoena within 10 court days of entry of this order, or later if the parties mutually agree. The parties shall meet and confer about the date of deposition within five court days of entry of this order. If Santos through counsel does not meet and confer within that time, the City may reset the deposition pursuant to this order without clearing the date with Santos.

The City also seeks sanctions. CCP 2025.450(g)(1) states, "If a motion under subdivision (a) is granted, the court shall impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) in favor of the party who noticed the deposition and against the deponent or the party with whom the deponent is affiliated, unless the court finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." The court does not find substantial justification here and awards sanctions. The court finds the City's counsel's hourly rate reasonable but reduces the number of hours to reflect that no opposition was filed and thus no reply brief was required. Santos shall pay $5990.71 to the City within 10 court days of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Atty. HUNTER SIMS III, Esq. of The City Attorney's Office (tel: 415-554-3853) for the Plaintiffs.
Defendant KEVIN O'CONNOR, in pro per present.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT KEVIN O'CONNOR's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs' City And County Of San Francisco And People Of The State Of California'S Discovery Responses.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The court denies without prejudice Defendant Kevin O'Connor's motion to compel the City and County of San Francisco to provide further responses to discovery. O'Connor has not provided a separate statement in support of the motion to compel (see California Rules of Court, Rule 3.1345(a)). This is more than a technicality; separate statements help the court quickly focus on the discovery in dispute and the basis for each party's position. O'Connor has also not complied with the statutory notice period of Code of Civil Procedure 1005(b) ("Unless otherwise ordered or specifically provided by law, all moving and supporting papers shall be served and filed at least 16 court days before the hearing."). O'Connor may cure these defects in a new motion.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter reflecting compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Defendant Peter Schurman'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Stay
Off Calendar Oct-07-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT PETER SCHURMAN Defendant Peter Schurman'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Stay is denied in part and granted in part. Defendant must file an answer to the first amended complaint, which need not be verified. (Code Civ. Proc. Section 446(a) [when a public entity is plaintiff, "the answer shall be verified, unless an admission of the truth of the complaint might subject the party to a criminal prosecution"].) DeCamp v. First Kensington Corporation (1978) 83 Cal.App.3d 268 is not to the contrary. (See id. at 276 [noting that "a 1933 amendment limited the self-incrimination exception so as to apply only to cases in which the state or an officer thereof was plaintiff," and holding only that "in private litigation the defendant is not entitled to defend a verified complaint unless he can deny its essential allegations under penalty of perjury" (emphasis added)].) The court grants as unopposed a limited stay of proceedings as to defendant until February 15, 2021. If defendant is still subject to criminal prosecution at that time, he may renew his motion.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
